title: oldenlandia corymbosa var. microcarpa
intro: Oldenlandia corymbosa var. microcarpa, also known as the small-fruited oldenlandia, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant activities. Oldenlandia corymbosa var. microcarpa can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
